column36: Comments

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Carol took off and refused to land

Brought in Black Panther to help enable Doctor Strange. He was even better than I thought he would be thanks to the scheme. Fight the Future was was the MVP with generating a lot of early attack to keep the city clear and prevent wounds to master strikes. Player 2 didn't get as lucky but still had a pretty good blue/green deck with Captain Marvel and Ms. America. The last turn for Player 1 was huge thanks to a ton of draws from Sorcerer Supreme, sidekicks and Black Panther. Had enough Cosmic Threat to hit The Beyonder once for free plus enough natural attack and recruit to finish him off and clear the city. 

Second game I gave up when I had an escape on turn 6 after a chain of twists. It made me realize how lucky I'd been on the first game with Fight the Future and early master strikes.
The final melking
Nice setup! Thanks for the tournament, and see you next time!

First game did not work out at all. Finally won with 1 villain card left and 3 escaped. Second game went significantly better after taking black panther out to bring in some extra demonic bargain cards with The Vishanti. Second game had a way better shuffle.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Beyonce, sorry Beyonder is no joke, went down to last twist, and only escaped it by pushing it down with danger sense few times. If HQ gave better start could do better, but no time and energy for second game. Great end of tournament

Full ranged deck on one side, and Instinct on the other! Scored 3 rares(Strange, Nova and Ms America) on the ranged side. Managed to hit the Beyonder twice in a single turn(Once with Cosmic Threat ranged from Nova's rare, and the other with raw attack)!

Very challenging setup, barely eeked out a draw in the first game with 9 escapes and 4 hits of the mastermind. The heroes made two clear decks but just weren't powerful enough to do damage fast enough. Second choices were much better though. The ancient one was a great choice with 9 5+ cost cards and the ability to fight the mastermind with recruit. Ended up with a draw your whole deck on one side which was fighting the mastermind every turn and another generally powerful instinct/range deck that was clearing the city. Awesome setup very down to the wire, thanks so much guys for designing it and all of them! It's been an awesome tournament!
Anyone who didn’t pick Forge to go with Ant-Man is a sucker

Both tries started strong, but stalled out in the midgame. I had enough attack to keep the city easily clear, but couldn't hit ZGG. I didn't see enough recruit cards to get the 5+ cost cards until after turn 20. I think I could have cut 8-10 turns off of my result with a better HQ.

This setup had four main threats. Brood herring stronger, covering zombies, mastermind potentially getting stronger and discarding cards, lots of wounds.  Found that Apoc Kitty Pride may come over HQ management, provide some heal via KO and has decent damage. Maximus was included to defeat Brood for free and with uncommon card always have an option to cover zombies. Was considering x23, and also piercing energy. 

First game was quite sluggish. Lots of villains, trap, so on. From turn 15 schemes appeared but was not that bad. Had some heals. With kitty could avoid master strikes except the one that scans the hero deck. It was turn 39 win, no escaped, slightly higher VP per turn. 

Second game cards came out better. Still in the end Hand2 had 6 or 8 wounds in a deck of 15 cards. So had to basically pass/heal for last three turns. Maximus was quite useful. Got his rare, which resulted in some extra KO. Ended up just ko-ing the final starting card and drawing full deck of 9 cards in last turn. Epic way to finish the Tournament.  

Dark descendants were more annoying than zombies. But did not meet them that much in second play. In first game I faced 5 zombies, and Maximus uncomment indeed helped covering two or three of them. Which is good on one hand. But also VP could have been higher with some stronger targets. 

I guess I will have quite good score on number of turns but not so great on vp. We will see. Anyway intriguing setup, I will remember this scheme.
I got lucky and the only rare that popped up in my winning game was Ant-Man's which I was able to snarf quickly with size-changing.  A great setup.  Great tournament!

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Ridley Scott alien vibes alert! We had fun with this. X-23 was a great addition. “Bioengineered assassin” was a great card to destroy the “scheme eggs”. Magik is the bomb with her side kick synergies. Ant man went gonzo with “risky science” and “ride the ants” which usually was able to cycle out wounds for more useful cards.  Most all cards of the HQ had attack values on them which helped with Berserk abilities as well. Has fun this tournament as usual. Major props to Bagel for another great time!

Pizza just kept getting grabbed the Brood infections so he had to skip a couple turns to ko them. Extra Cheese was able to beset to average 12 to 16 attack each hand near the end after KOing all his starter cards.

We had fun again as we have all tournament. We were happy with our hero choices so stayed with them for the second game - thinking we might be able to play even better - though our second game was very very close. One turn longer for .2 PPT difference- hopefully we picked the right game. Though we feel we still took a bit too long, we lost some of our Rare's which made us have to discard more cards so had some weak turns as well as getting scheme twists out early giving us extra wounds we had to heal. Eventually though Wolverine and Domino did their job and slayed everybody. Getting the hang of when to scan the cards took us a few extra turns as well. We have very much enjoyed this tournament and thank you Bagel for all your work and look forward to next one and are happy we have ended above the halfway mark.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36:
I hoped Bishop and Banshee would pair well and they really did. Player 2 recruited so many and had no trouble with the growing brood henchmen, and Bishop was able to KO the schemes in the discard pile! I found that out a little too late and Player 1 ended up with a bit too many wounds before I realized this. 
Player 1 got too many wounds and just couldn't get enough recruit. I noticed Blade, Ant-Man and Colossus&Wolverine all had attack icons so I was hoping to build a berserk deck, but I didn't get the cards for it. It was a lot more blade than the other two. So it wasn't a berserk deck it ended up a move around enemies deck, but couldn't generate MM level of attack. 
Player 2 meanwhile got two hits twice, building attack and PE. 
I wish it could've happened a little faster, but PE takes a little time to get to MM amounts.

I liked this setup.  I had some great beast/Ant-man draw combos and all the berserk with C/W and beast led to some great turns.  Took me a long time to get the wind though because I lost two 7+ cards to zombie green goblin early on the game.

Got inundated with wounds the first game and just not enough ways to deal with them. Having them going to the top of the deck really put a damper on some things. Second game I ran into far less wound creating cards and Bishop was able to effectively thin out one of the hands to combine with War Machine's hyperspeed. Shout out to Skid for helping with the wounds.
Brought Strong Guy to support Multiple Man. Then brought Warpath to A) bring more 2 costs for Elektra B) synergize with Speed's 4 cost common super well, and C) Uncommon makes huge hands!

The most frustrating thing about hero cloning is when you shuffle the hero deck and you see there was a rare card on the top or close to getting into the HQ :( It happened more than once for me. I only saw 2 rares in the two games combined, which was bad. The henchmen worked great, the chivalrous duels made sometimes hard to keep the city clean. I actually managed to survive all the Master Strikes without a single wound in the first game, that was pretty great. Great game, super fun tournament!
Failed game one almost drew a bomb on the winning turn that would have ended the game. Very stressful final turn but very fun win.

Prioritized MK heroes which was helpful and it never hurts when you have MVP Elektra. One hand was super optimized and got all the points. Couldn't improve it the second time even with a little more fight / KO. Thanks for all the behind the scenes work for the tournament!

This scheme is a mess. Within the first 60% we had drawn all scheme twists. In both games. Gave us a loss in the first game. The scheme is a unnecessary time bomb. The rest of the deck has no impact.
We've got the feeling, that we picked the wrong heroes, but in the end, Speed escalated hard and gave as the win.
The worst setup we ever played, 1 star because of the scheme and our bad card draw. Nothing seems to matter.

Found lots of fun interactions with these heroes. Multiple Man loves Star Lord, and Hyperspeed revealing cards meant Warpath was happy too! Elektra just hung around with a First Strike and (eventually) a Sai Blades each to stop Strikes doing anything. 
I think I enjoyed it more than my wife, as I picked up Warpath's Rare in game 2 and ruled the roost with it. Having 4 extra cards and an implanted memory chip led to some massive turns! I went full into Hyperspeed game 1 but that wasn't quite as good despite turning my deck over every turn.
She went Star Lord and Multiple Man both times, but didn't Clone a single card in game 1. Game 2 she got his Rares and KOd more cards so enjoyed that game more. 😁

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Fun opportunity to use some new cards in my collection from Messiah Complex! Warpath and Speed provided satisfying synergy with the top of the deck. Multiple Man was unique to try. Didn't end up KO-ing as many cards with its rares as Warpath did for the other player. I had a nice turn where I could draw 2 wounds from the top of my deck to add to the 2 which started in my hand to efficiently KO 4 wounds in one turn. Messiah Complex is complex with keywords, but it's been fun with a lot of variety! Great tournament! Cheers Bagel!

Definitely quite a hard set up, got very close to losing both times, the scheme is very luck dependent. Decided since this was the last pod I'd try two different sets of hero choices - one with Daredevil and Warpath which has some synergy with the existing heroes, and one with just raw power in Bishop and Iceman. Game 1 went alright, got quite lucky with the scheme twists, but wasn't much KO so I built up quite slow. Game 2, the bishop/iceman side worked very well but my other deck which I was trying to build Multiple Man just couldn't get started as there weren't any of his cards in the HQ.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: I got very lucky with both fateful resurrection and HQ

I picked up Telepathic Mastery early and then got two copies of Dark Foretelling, one for each deck, so Fateful Resurrection and obnoxious charges were a non-issue. Then I abused the Scheme to get nice trim decks by sending tons of cards Undercover. Left-hand deck picked up Ghost Rider’s rare and managed to play it three times in one turn with Stingray’s draw cards and a sidekick. It was just a case of mopping up points after that. Dr Punisher’s presidential campaign ended up tied for votes with Shiklah’s while Stingray was close behind as a third party. Second game came close but wasn’t quite as good.

A bit trickier than I had first anticipated. I thought first the strategy was to always discard to prevent the MM votes, but I soon realized that sometimes you want votes to happen to get rid of Twists and sometimes you want to discard to reshuffle the Villain Deck. Pretty tricky.

Ghost Rider and Stingray go brr. There is enough card draw between them in the last game to cycle through my entire deck which also had the Black Swan Rare to get up to 27 attack and clear the way from scheme twists to hit the Mastermind over and over again.

The other hand didn't have enough time to come together as they were throwing Dr. Punisher for presidency to keep the game going.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Great setup to end the season on.  Having Silk as a hero option and showing up was huge.  See you next season!!!

Fun setup, I have tried this scheme a couple of times before but got a better appreciation for how to play it.  I finally realized that you can play a hero during a turn and THEN stack them to the scheme - much smarter :)   The first attempt my candidate was Ghost Rider 2099.  Player 1 got 3 undercover Range to take full advantage of Doom 2099's attack power, while player 2 just kept up with nominating Ghost.  I got a little KO-happy with Player 1, and had to scrounge for some extra recruit during the last several turns. 
In the second game, I switched things up with Corvus and Devil.  I never got much of a KO'd bystander engine running, but the low-cost recruit cards and spectrum abilities were real handy, allowing both players to ramp up pretty solid attack and recruiting.  Dr. Punisher took the nomination and kept up in the election no problem, more villain deck foresight was super-useful, and in the end a more efficient and effective win in Game 2.

First attempt had two 4-2 split opening hands, which led to a lot of early pressure on the City and with the Resurrections and Sapiens led to two escapes.  I hoped to do better on a second go, but Shiklah just kept stuffing the ballot box faster than I could turn out voters to the polls.

Awesome setup! I liked the Scheme to begin with and the Villains and Mastermind were great choices to really bung things up for me. LOL. I went double-Beast as I felt I could fend off the twists a little better (and did) and he worked great in a tech-based side. Felt I could've been faster as I got caught with a couple resurrections when attacking the MM, but managed to keep things pretty tidy. Second attempt was a huge mess with early rares clogging up my HQ and no Beasts showing up. I'm going to try this one again before I pack it away. Another great example of how this game can feel fresh each and every time you pull cards out of the box.

I went for a fun setup, wanting to get a Gwen card drunk on her bystander power. I had nearly all of the special bystanders to look forward to. I picked Spider-Gwen for her bystander rescue common that also powered her up, and so I would have a cheap hero to throw at the scheme. Elsa also had a common that rescued bystanders, and her yellows looked like they would work well with Black Swan.

In the first game I made the mistake of letting the first 2 twists trigger instead of discarding to negate them. Between the master strikes and regular twists I lost on turn 15 with 7 twists out. I won the second game, but it was slow, and I let 3 villains escape. My dream of having President Gwen one shot all of the mastermind tactics was foiled by needing to put nearly all of her cards into the scheme. I did end up with a lot of bystanders, and if the game had gone one more turn I would have finally had a 16 power Gwen card, but Stingray's rare ended up finishing the game a turn early.

column2: Escape From Big Trouble in the Galaxy Vol. 2
column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: This was my first time playing against Ego, the Expansion of the city and eventually the contraction of the city was as fun as I've heard. The interaction with the sealing of city spaces from the scheme was really cool. I definitely liked having something to spend excess attack on. The false Aesir kept stealing Ravager weapons which made it hard to take them down, but I eventually had to face them to get rid of the extra city spaces. Definitely recommended this setup!

It's tough for any Ego setup to be anything but a 4 or 5 for me. This scheme is awesome with Ego- I really liked how it was a benefit with a large city, but dangerous with a small one. Banner and Cassie took one deck and absolutely dominated.

First off, I really, really didn't like this week's setup. It's only by recruiting 3 very good rares for Deck 1 that I ended up winning in the end and the well played supporting role from Deck 2. I really did not like the combo of False Aesir and Ego. Technically the Quantumnauts were at least normal KO Henchmen, but they were both too strong (because of the full CIty) and too diluted becausse of an extra Villain Group. The City was very hard to handle well, so in attempt 1, even though it went well from halfway through, I still had 3 escapes happen. The combination of Ego and this Scheme was also not very fun to me, but that might just be beacuse I dislike the Scheme anyway.

So despite all these dislikes, I did end up winning attempt 1. Attempt 2 completely went as I imagined this diabolical setup going beforehand. I got a lot of wounds and almost always could just barely attack something in the CIty, or not even that. Mantis felt like a bad hero in general, but she actually played an important role in attempt 1, by getting 3 of her Sefless/Selfish cards into Deck 2 (I guess because of the (3/3/3/2/2/1) distribution that were all the copies), so that Deck 1, which did end up KOing a lot of starters eventually, could draw extra cards and do the fighting that deck 2 mostly just couldn't. The scores of 68 vs 12 probably reflects that and I think at least 3 of those VP were from Undercover cards as well.

The cards that ended up bringing me the win were Black Panther's 5-cost supplemented by other Black Panther cards (a few of the 'draw a card' 2-cost) that can convert its recruit to attack after a strength trigger, Hulk 2099's rare, Cassie Lang's rare and a bunch of Janet van Dyne's cards, as well as her rare. So much Explore ended up happening that I saw the cards that went to the bottom of the Hero Deck starting to reappear again towards the final turns. I made 52 attack in turn 29, which was enough to end it with the CIty situation at that moment.

Loki 2099 was annoyingly shuffling a Master Strike back in once, which probably did come out again, as 5 were totally played with1 left in the Deck, but at least it didn't happen twice or was played as a result of his Fight or Fail effect. The Ravagers were quite strong in this setup, as they were commanding with no other Crew around mostly, but with the rares early on I could get the strong enough turns going. Hulk 2099's Strength common was invaluable as well, sending the many wounds from Thor 2099 and the Master Strikes undercover. Microscopic Size-Changing did help a few times to get both cards from HQ cheaper and defeat several Villains for reduced numbers.

All in all, I think I got very lucky to even win this setup. If attempt 1 was more like attempt 2, I would have simply struggled to defeat anything at all and could also probably not recruit any strong enough cards. My morale was already pretty low in attempt 1 as I could not prevent all escapes, but attempt 2 went so much worse that I think I didn't even know how lucky I had been just yet. Preventing a loss from the Scheme is also too luck-based in my opininon, and in attempt 2 it indeed wasn't preventable.

Thanks to Bageltop Games for organizing the Tournament, it was a lot of fun, even with some of the setups being on the more difficult and less fun side. Some (parts) of these setups I would never come up with by nyyself, as I'd find them too punishing and I mostly just want to have fun when I play. This was a nice chance to get somewhat out of that comfort zone (as is also the case with the Randomizer) and do play the setups I'd never come up with myself. Also the competitive part is a big part of the enjoyment and although I didn't do nearly as well as last tournament I did try my best and that's enough.

A lot of stuff I'd never actually played with before.  Lots to keep track of.  I figured since it was the last round and i wasn't anywhere near the top of the rankings, I just picked my 2 heroes randomly.  Then I picked two different heroes randomly for the second game since it didn't matter how many I burned through at this point.  Now that the tournament is over, need to find a new way to play. :)
My only escape came from Ego's final tactic -_- Apart from that, this was a killer game. Wanda & Vision paired up so well with Mantis and we got over 30 attack on two separate turns!

Very interesting puzzle to figure out for sure. One went mother daughter combo with Janet and Wasp and the other went heavy on range and tech, second round went much better, both times able to cycle through and get all the rates as well

Whoa Nelly! This one was a really tough challenge with so much going on; expanding city, needing to keep seals in play, ambush schemes, uru enchanted weapons, I needed a co-pilot just to keep it all straight. After 15 turns, I didn't think I had a chance, but the Dr. Doom 2099 Cyber Mod strategy started to pay off big and helped me get control of the board.

column36: Great scenario. Well balanced and lots of fun!
I don't know how you win this game without Danger sense. Yondu's Uncommon came in clutch

We took the Captain & the Devil for some Spectrum activation, and Blade Daywalker to help move Villains in the city, and they performed pretty well. For me, Nebula was doing a lot of nice things, including KO and Tech triggers. Without us needing to try, the Sewers always seemed to be clear, so Elsa was rescuing many free Bystanders for us, which was a boon with the stacked Bystander deck. We won, but the setup was still a challenge, and we cursed together when a Master Strike or Whizzer showed up at the wrong time. We had a fun time overall with the tournament, thanks for setting everything up! =)

Had frustrating escapes with both games which is what this setup was designed to do, so I chose the game with less PPT and less escapes.  Last week so I wanted to try two different teams to see which was better, if I had a third chance I think DayBlade and Phyla-Vell might've been a stronger team-up.  Comic side stacked Instinct cards and kept the city clear while MCU side leaned on quick recruiting and Spectrum to grab expensive cards.  Hyperion had 4 Strikes played with a clear city and I thought I was going to win with 0 escapes but a couple of bad hands led to him pushing out both Doctor Spectrums and a bystander taking both hands down to three cards, delaying the win by a few turns.

I'll be honest, this one wasn't as fun. Fist game i lost in 24 turns due to 9 Twists and an escaped Nighthawk. When tearing down, i found the last twist 2nd card from the bottom '-'
Second game i would have lost in two more turns from the same circumstance. And on game 2 both Whizzers came out in a full city. Beast was surely the MVP though
This was such an annoying experience, especially with the Rare that I was going to buy super early into the game getting feasted the turn it was going to get bought. Overall, not fun

column36: Decks just never got working until the end of the game. All the tools were there but they never fell together as we could never get the KO engine running

Rough ending to the tournament, it was just too tough to get ahead. Utopolis and Hyperion just cause too many escapes, each villain group can put in another Mastermind, and when you hit Hyperion in the city it doesn't get rid of a tactic. Add in the tokens from the scheme, shards, and haunts and there's just a lot of little bits to track. It was like all of my least favorite parts of legendary rolled into one setup. I tried leaning in on tech and spectrum cards in game one, then on shards in game two, but I just couldn't make enough to get even a single Hyperion tactic.
Very difficult because of all the villains that “push” and having to spend attack to move in the city. Building shards was an added frustration. Yikes!

column36: This setup scheme is so hard. I got so lucky early on with twists coming out when I still had plenty of starters. But as we got closer to the end I would have one player consistently draw and have to KO a non grey hero. I had 7 at game end. ONE more and I would have lost.
Fun.
Star-lord's rare is super busted. Fun combination of heroes with Lady Sif having good synergy with Star-lord.

Found this week frustrating and couldn’t get my deck going. Felt like I could have finished the game a little quicker on my first attempt if it wasn’t for twists making me KO some of my attack cards, including Mockingbirds rare. 
Overall, really enjoyed the tournament and can’t wait until the next!

Two almost identical games-- both went the same number of turns, and in the end were only one point apart in total VP.  I'm moderately pleased to have won this fairly challenging setup at all, but it wasn't the strong finish I was hoping for.

Hated the first game I pulled a DNF and packed up. Between this and the sceme last week, I’m totally burnt out on this game, which hasn’t happened before.
Despite the escapes, the second game went well and Mockingbird carried the whole game (with some help from Yelena).
First try featured early STs and so we were able to dodge the worst of the Scheme. Yelena and Gamora led the two hands and were able to steadily hit Thanos once the Gems came out.
N/A

Ooof, that was rough! in both attempts the deck building was really working where Yelena and Sif worked with one deck and Beta Ray Bill cards aplenty for the other one. Just as the decks were nearing unstoppable levels, Thanos's Master Strike and the Sniper Rifle Assassins seemed to take out key cards that severely hampered our deck synergy. Bye bye all three Stormbreakers, I barely got to throw thee!

A wonderful tourney, thanks again to Bagel, Kylah, and the entire community!

Moon Knight is one of my favorite heroes, so I always enjoy trying out new combos with him. I picked Iron Fist to try to maximize the Outwit ability and I kinda picked Wolverine X-Force on a whim. I did not expect these 5 heroes to synergize so well together. I had some amazing turns (at least 4 above 20 attack and the last hand at 54 attack). Some unfortunate timing with the master strikes set me back at some key moments (I discarded three rare cards between both hands once). With some more luck, I probably could have finished faster. But I'm happy with it.
Villains' setup felt like a nice level of difficulty. A great game and a great way to finish out the tourney.

Switched to a different strategy on second game with less draw heros and it paid off. Hulk was King as was able to generate lots of attack from Smash and then Wounded Fury with The Great Scar by the end of the game. Being able to reuse Hulk is King for discards (checked rules because thought it must be wrong) helped make Illuminati Open Warfare less punishing. Nice way to end the tourney. Thanks to all who set it up.

My first attempt started out great, but then all the draw cards I had with Wolverine and Amadeus Cho backfired on me when the Mastermind transformed. It made for a slow game. Second game I decided to sort of ignore draw cards and went with Quicksilver and Speed so I could hyperspeed everything. I could have had better luck in the HQ, but the strategy finally worked out for me. I had a couple of turns with attack in the 20s because of all the hyperspeed. All the henchmen and feasting helped me thin my decks, which was really useful.
Domino is a pretty great hero! managed to get one deck to have 1 base card and 4 rares, all except amadeus cho's rare.  The other deck was very sad collecting wounds and KOing nothing :(

column36: I only had time for a quick game and this setup came through! Only one escape feels not too bad for this scheme, it was an unavoidable henchman to scheme twist combo so honestly this is a great way to end the tournament. Angel does WORK. Got so many bystanders and card draws from his ability. Also got Joe Fixit's rare early on so him and Angel did a ton of damage and recruiting really quickly. My other side had a super solid SHIELD thing going on, but I was already ready to win by the time it was setup so this side mostly just cleared stuff in the city. Not too bad! Excited to see other's hero choices. I was really close to going with Gladiator Hulk instead but I'm happy where I landed even if I didn't see much Lady Thor.

I think I finally had a game where all the heroes played had good synergy.  A very enjoyable game even though we twice had a Goon pulled with the next card being a Twist so we had no chance of stopping the Goon from escaping.

column36: My fastest game yet. I did have the bad habit of every time a goon came out the card that followed it was a scheme twist so a lot more villains escaped than what I would have liked but I think the overall score of the game was a pretty good one.

Solid fun game. And if I'm being honest, it was a little more fun because of how lucky we were on both games. First game went really well, to the point where we were sure our second game was going to be much worse, but it actually turned out way better. The first 4 henchmen that came out were directly followed by a scheme twist, not only making as bad, but also keeping the city cleared up. Then we were able to keep the goons out before other twists. The last twist came out after our 4th goon escaped and no goons in the city, so then we were able to laser focus on the mastermind. We were expecting both games to be brutal (after reading the setup), but we got extremely lucky in villain shuffles. Which I'm glad, because we have had so many BAD games this tournament, unfortunately. Glad we were able to at least go out with a solid game.
Hand 1 just did all the heavy lifting  really with Iceman and Quake working well. Couldn't get Logan into the game to support the rest.

column36: Very fun. Got hit With some schemes early, but recovered, well great synergy with Jean Gray rescuing bystanders in Mockingbird getting her level up

First, I got to use my hologram Crime Wave card.  Awesome.  Second, I hate organized crime wave.  A scheme I love to hate though.  My Wife decided to play and son sat in to watch and they had never experienced the special torture that is organized crime wave.  It was great fun teaching them (and first time using the Shield set since I got it).  I plan to replay it with some other options (more recruit) like Lady Thor who was unavailable.  I know I seriously underperformed this tourney, but someone has to be at the bottom.  It was a TON of fun.  Second attempt I didn't scheme out and ran out the villain deck instead.  Couldn't really get my hands going enough to control city and hit Nimrod.
A lot of fun.

Fun setup, but man was there a lot of shuffling! Luke Cage Noir is very fun, coupled w/ Strong Guy. Both games the hand that recruited those cards did the best. My attempt at Siryn and Warpath didn't work so well. I found Tactical Formation very hard to pull off. I think I only got it once between both games. In the end I had to choose between a shorter game with more escapes and less VP vs a longer game with less escapes and more VP. Hope I made the right choice!

Thank you Bagel for another great tournament!!

Brent says: "Early Sinister Clones are a bummer for tournament play when you get the typical 4/2 starting hands, but at least game 2 only had 1 escape instead of the 6 I had game 1. A fun setup where I had to slow down and really consider most turns."
